executionHistory.summary: Reconciled the documentation-only UX contract with a closed human-readable readiness vocabulary, managed-document summary and detail patterns, correction and evidence-age presentation, accessible narrow-view behavior, author-facing overclaim diagnostics, browser-visible managed-document assertions, and routed design/docs questions. No application UI, managed-doc edit, runtime evidence, implementation, test execution, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, or deployment is claimed.

Replaced the design-routing stub with a source-grounded canonical capability-readiness architecture: explicit compiled catalog and source ownership; append-only PostgreSQL evidence, corrections, and immutable snapshots; independent implemented/configured/activated/healthy/journey-verified/deployed facts with degraded/disabled overlays; one deterministic freshness and claim resolver; privacy-safe BUG-102-001 and deploy-result ingestion; shared docs, status, settings, navigation, and spec 106 projections; and explicit READY-001..012 mechanism mapping. Artifact lint and artifact-freshness guard passed. The read-only traceability guard remained red because the plan-owned scopes reference scenario ranges instead of containing executable Gherkin blocks; no foreign artifact was edited. No spec, scope, bug, report, user validation, scenario manifest, source, test, managed-doc, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, runtime, production, or deployment change is claimed.

Finalized a five-scope executable planning DAG for the canonical capability catalog/resolver, append-only PostgreSQL evidence, immutable snapshots and claim lint, shared runtime projections, and BUG-102 journey-evidence ingestion with a docs-owner publication handoff. Reconciled scenario-manifest and 32-row test-plan handoffs; artifact lint and traceability passed. No requirements, design, source, tests, managed docs, runtime, adapter, production data, certification, commit, push, or deployment change is claimed.

Reconciled GRILL-02 and SR-012: BUG-102 is the immutable acceptance-evidence producer, BUG-032 is the readiness/claims consumer, and spec 106 is the presentation consumer. Spec 104 remains top-level blocked; only completed-scope evidence is eligible for bounded claims. Preserved SCN-032-004-01..09 and added 10..11. No source, tests, design, scopes, report, managed docs, certification, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

Spec-scope-hardening reconciliation after the 2026-07-24T05:46Z independent-review spec revision. Integrated the two newly added spec Gherkin scenarios SCN-032-004-10 (Acceptance evidence has one producer) and SCN-032-004-11 (Blocked spec status is not promoted by completed scopes) into scenario-manifest.json (9->11), test-plan.json (TP-032-05-09/10), and scopes.md SCOPE-05 (Gherkin, Test Plan rows, Core Outcomes, Test Evidence Parity 8->10). Verified the 102-001 -> 032-004 -> 106 chain edge: SCOPE-05 Depends On 'BUG-102-001 SCOPE-05', and state.json evidenceLifecycleRole (readiness_consumer; acceptanceProducer BUG-102-001; presentationConsumer spec 106; producerDependencyOnThisPacket false) plus specDependsOn on BUG-102-001 are present and correct. RESIDUAL routed to bubbles.design: design.md (2026-07-23T21:11Z) predates the 05:46Z spec revision, still states 'satisfy READY-001 through READY-012', and has zero spec-104 coverage, so READY-014 / SCN-032-004-11 lack a grounded design section; SCN-11 was planned from the current spec.md + state.json dependencyEvidencePolicy pending a design refresh. Status held in_progress; no source, tests, managed docs, ledger, runtime, certification, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed, and no foreign packet (102/106/104/079) was edited.

Reconciled design.md to the 2026-07-24T05:46Z spec revision. Extended Purpose/Scope from 'READY-001 through READY-012' to READY-001 through READY-014; added the '### Dependency Spec Status And Bounded Scope Evidence (READY-014)' subsection under Deterministic Resolution grounding the spec-104 blocked-status derivation (dependency spec status read exact-from-disk; a top-level blocked dependency spec is NOT promoted by individually completed scopes; completed Scope 8 evidence affirms only the bounded Assistant journey it proves; forbidden claim 'spec 104 done'/'fully ready'), realizing the packet's dependencyEvidencePolicy; mapped READY-013 (one-way BUG-102 producer -> readiness consumer -> spec 106 presentation, no loop) and READY-014 in the Requirement-To-Mechanism table; added SCN-032-004-10 and SCN-032-004-11 to the design Scenario Mapping table. artifact-lint PASSED (exit 0); traceability-guard PASSED (exit 0, 11 scenarios, 11 report-evidence refs) unchanged from baseline. NO follow-up plan route required for scope/test coverage: SCN-10/11 were already integrated by the prior bubbles.plan harden pass into scopes.md/scenario-manifest.json/test-plan.json (plan-owned; not edited here). No spec, scope, scenario-manifest, test-plan, source, test, managed-doc, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, deployment, or foreign packet (102/104/106/079) change is claimed.
